name: TigerGraph Graph Database API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade native parallel graph database with REST++ API — enabling organizations to analyze massive graph datasets at scale with GSQL (graph-native query language), machine learning workloads on graphs, and real-time deep link analytics, with TigerGraph Cloud for managed hosting and self-hosted enterprise deployment.
use_cases: Running deep link analytics via TigerGraph API for fraud detection and network analysis
Executing graph machine learning via TigerGraph for recommendation engines and entity resolution
Querying large-scale graphs via TigerGraph GSQL API for supply chain and risk analytics
Building real-time graph applications via TigerGraph REST API for financial crime detection
Loading and querying graph data via TigerGraph for customer 360 and identity graphs
not_for: Simple property graphs with moderate scale (use Neo4j for developer-friendly Cypher queries)
RDF/SPARQL semantic knowledge graphs (use Stardog or Amazon Neptune for semantic web)
Small datasets without graph complexity (use PostgreSQL with recursive CTEs for simple graphs)
best_when: An enterprise data team needs to run complex graph analytics on large-scale datasets (billions of edges) with distributed parallel processing — especially fraud detection, supply chain, or financial crime analytics.
avoid_when: You need developer-friendly Cypher queries, small-to-medium graphs, or open-source without enterprise licensing.

auth.notes: OAuth2 password grant for token. POST /requesttoken with username and password → JWT access_token. Authorization: Bearer {access_token}. Token expiry: 1 hour (configurable). Self-hosted: user management via GSQL. TigerGraph Cloud: API token from console. REST++ endpoint: {server}:14240/restpp/. Cloud: {tenant}.i.tgcloud.io:443/. Graph-specific paths: /graph/{graph_name}/. Python SDK: pyTigerGraph. GSQL: shell/web console for schema and query definition.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 50GB RAM, 1 instance (TigerGraph Cloud); Developer edition: free
pricing.notes: US company (Redwood City, CA). Founded 2012 by Yu Xu (UC San Diego professor). $170M+ funding (Tiger Global, DCVC, Wells Fargo). TigerGraph Developer Edition: free, unlimited time, non-production. TigerGraph Cloud: cloud-hosted. Enterprise: on-premises with support. GSQL (Graph Structured Query Language): native TigerGraph query language — proprietary but powerful. Parallel graph processing: distributed parallel computation for deep link analytics. Graph ML: built-in graph neural network (GNN) and PageRank algorithms. Certifications: SOC2, ISO27001. Used by: Wish, UnitedHealth, JPMorgan Chase, Intuit. Fraud detection: primary use case. Graph algorithms: 50+ built-in (PageRank, Louvain, Betweenness Centrality, etc.).

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; depends on instance size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 password grant for JWT. REST++ endpoint (port 14240). GSQL query language required. 1-hour token expiry. Graph-specific paths. pyTigerGraph SDK. Cloud vs self-hosted different endpoints. Enterprise pricing. Free developer edition.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: REST++ GET queries idempotent. Graph data loads: use upsert for idempotent vertex/edge insertion. Installed queries: calling same query with same params is idempotent for reads.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 password grant: POST credentials to /requesttoken — not standard OAuth2 client credentials
GSQL required: queries must be written in GSQL and installed (compiled) before REST++ calls
Graph name in path: /restpp/graph/{graph_name}/ required for all graph operations
REST++ port 14240: different from GSQL port (14241) — ensure correct port for REST API
TigerGraph Cloud vs self-hosted: significantly different endpoint URLs and authentication
GSQL compile step: new queries must be GSQL-compiled and installed before execution via REST

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 JWT with 1-hour expiry. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. GDPR compliant. Enterprise graph data (fraud networks, financial transactions) is highly sensitive. Self-hosted: operator responsible for network security. REST++ port (14240) should not be public-facing. pyTigerGraph handles token refresh. Row-level access control via GSQL roles. Data masking: not natively supported — implement at application layer.
